466:     # Returns a cache version that can be used together with the cache key to form
467:     # a recyclable caching scheme. The cache version is built with the number of records
468:     # matching the query, and the timestamp of the last updated record. When a new record
469:     # comes to match the query, or any of the existing records is updated or deleted,
470:     # the cache version changes.
471:     #
472:     # If the collection is loaded, the method will iterate through the records
473:     # to generate the timestamp, otherwise it will trigger one SQL query like:
474:     #
475:     #    SELECT COUNT(*), MAX("products"."updated_at") FROM "products" WHERE (name like '%Cosmic Encounter%')
476:     def cache_version(timestamp_column = :updated_at)
477:       if model.collection_cache_versioning
478:         @cache_versions ||= {}
479:         @cache_versions[timestamp_column] ||= compute_cache_version(timestamp_column)
480:       end
481:     end

484:       timestamp_column = timestamp_column.to_s
485: 
486:       if loaded?

490:         end

497:
